method_atom: a randomized algorithm that computes a near-optimal low-rank approximation of a large linear operator by projecting it onto a small random subspace, achieving accuracy provably close to the best attainable rank-deficient approximation (in the spectral norm) with efficiency independent of the operator's dimensions.
object_atom: the data matrix in principal component analysis, whose dominant low-rank structure encodes the leading directions of variation in a high-dimensional dataset.
brief_reason: the transferable machinery is randomized-projection low-rank approximation of a matrix with near-optimal spectral-norm accuracy guarantees; the domain-specific object is the PCA data matrix and its principal components.
